headline: The rebuilding of Iran's ballistic arsenal challenges claims of a decisive US victory
description: Investigations by 'The New York Times' and 'The Washington Post' say that Iran has managed to preserve part of its missile stockpiles and launchers.

Cuba's small businesses turn to solar energy to offset fuel shortages
description: Nearly four months after Donald Trump imposed an oil blockade, the island continues to sink deeper into a severe energy crisis. With power outages increasing, the government is betting on photovoltaic energy with support from China.

headline: UK PM Starmer's rivals make cautious moves
description: Although the UK prime minister has been fiercely criticized within Labour, none of his opponents have so far put themselves forward to replace him. The procedure to oust the leader is complex and risky.

headline: Cannes 2026: Park Chan-wook, jury president, says he is ready for 'fierce debates'
description: The elegant and discreet Korean filmmaker has retained from his years of opposition to dictatorship a taste for rebellion and debate.

headline: In the US and France, media moguls Ellison and Bolloré 'have no compunction about using their influence to serve far-right politics'
description: American historian David Bell discusses the 'worrisome' interventionism of French billionaire Vincent Bolloré at the Grasset publishing house. However, he considers 'the American situation to be more dire.'

description: Le Monde verified footage from Chinese social media and collected testimonies from victims of Beijing's crackdown to reveal how the regime has brought independent churches to heel since Xi Jinping came to power.

headline: After the 2025 disaster, Macron hopes to make his last year as president count
description: Emmanuel Macron, who will leave office on May 13, 2027, seeks to be remembered as a president who stayed active until the end. On the world stage, he recently regained some of the credit he lost to domestic challenges.
